edges.description: Reduced GTP cyclohydrolase I limits BH4 cofactor availability.
edges.target: BH4-Dependent Catecholamine and Phenylalanine Hydroxylation Impairment

edges.description: BH4 cofactor limitation reduces dopamine synthesis in motor circuits.
edges.source: BH4-Dependent Catecholamine and Phenylalanine Hydroxylation Impairment

edges.description: BH4-dependent phenylalanine handling can be transiently abnormal.
intermediate_mechanisms: reduced BH4 cofactor availability for aromatic amino-acid hydroxylases
edges.source: BH4-Dependent Catecholamine and Phenylalanine Hydroxylation Impairment

edges.description: Elevated blood phenylalanine reports the hyperphenylalaninemia branch.
intermediate_mechanisms: reduced BH4 cofactor availability for aromatic amino-acid hydroxylases
edges.source: BH4-Dependent Catecholamine and Phenylalanine Hydroxylation Impairment

edges.description: Orphanet records hearing impairment as frequent in autosomal dominant DRD, but the intermediary mechanism from BH4/GCH1 dysfunction is unresolved in the cached disease-specific sources.
edges.source: BH4-Dependent Catecholamine and Phenylalanine Hydroxylation Impairment

intermediate_mechanisms: chronic foot dystonia and abnormal lower-limb posture
chronic foot dystonia and flexion-inversion posture

edges.description: Low CSF HVA reports reduced central dopamine synthesis.
Elevated blood phenylalanine reports impaired BH4-dependent phenylalanine handling.
edges.source: BH4-Dependent Catecholamine and Phenylalanine Hydroxylation Impairment

nodes.description: Substantia nigra abnormalities are listed by Orphanet as frequent.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0045007 | Abnormality of the substantia nigra | Frequent (79-30%) |

nodes.description: Anxiety is listed as frequent.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0000739 | Anxiety | Frequent (79-30%) |

nodes.description: Ataxia is reported as frequent by Orphanet, but GeneReviews states that cerebellar disturbances generally do not occur in GTPCH1-deficient DRD; this discrepancy is preserved for future review.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0001251 | Ataxia | Frequent (79-30%) |
Intellectual, cerebellar, sensory, and autonomic disturbances generally do not occur.

nodes.description: Reduced tetrahydrobiopterin availability impairs BH4-dependent aromatic amino-acid hydroxylation, lowering catecholamine/dopamine synthesis and explaining the recurrent CSF homovanillic-acid and transient hyperphenylalaninemia readouts recorded for this disorder.
nodes.id: BH4-Dependent Catecholamine and Phenylalanine Hydroxylation Impairment

evidence.reference_title: Dopa-responsive dystonia in Chinese patients: Including a novel heterozygous mutation in the GCH1 gene with an intermediate phenotype and one case of prenatal diagnosis.
evidence.snippet: DRD is caused by the mutations in the genes encoding the enzymes involved in the dopamine and tetrahydrobiopterin (BH4) biosynthesis, including the GTP cyclohydrolase 1 (GCH1) gene and the tyrosine hydroxylase (TH) gene.
| HP:0008297 | Transient hyperphenylalaninemia | Frequent (79-30%) |

nodes.description: Babinski sign is frequent.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0003487 | Babinski sign | Frequent (79-30%) |
| HP:0008297 | Transient hyperphenylalaninemia | Frequent (79-30%) |

readouts.interpretation: Elevated blood phenylalanine reports impaired BH4-dependent phenylalanine handling.
readouts.target: BH4-Dependent Catecholamine and Phenylalanine Hydroxylation Impairment

nodes.description: Brisk reflexes are frequent, especially in the lower limbs.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0001348 | Brisk reflexes | Frequent (79-30%) |
Brisk deep-tendon reflexes in the legs, ankle clonus, and/or the striatal toe (dystonic extension of the big toe) are present in many affected individuals.
| HP:0003785 | Decreased CSF homovanillic acid concentration | Frequent (79-30%) |

readouts.interpretation: Low CSF HVA reports reduced central dopamine synthesis.

nodes.description: Decreased CSF homovanillic acid reflects impaired central dopamine metabolism and is listed by Orphanet as frequent.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0003785 | Decreased CSF homovanillic acid concentration | Frequent (79-30%) |

nodes.description: Depression is listed as frequent.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0000716 | Depression | Frequent (79-30%) |

nodes.description: Extrapyramidal motor dysfunction is frequent in this disorder.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0002071 | Abnormality of extrapyramidal motor function | Frequent (79-30%) |

nodes.description: Fatigue is listed as frequent.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0012378 | Fatigue | Frequent (79-30%) |

nodes.description: Focal dystonia is reported frequently in Orphanet.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0004373 | Focal dystonia | Frequent (79-30%) |

nodes.description: Heterozygous pathogenic variants in GCH1 reduce GTP cyclohydrolase I function, the first step of tetrahydrobiopterin synthesis, thereby limiting cofactor availability for dopamine biosynthesis.

evidence.reference_title: Dopa-responsive dystonia in British patients: new mutations of the GTP-cyclohydrolase I gene and evidence for genetic heterogeneity.
evidence.snippet: Recently the GTP cyclohydrolase I (GTPCH) gene was isolated as the first causative gene for dopa-responsive dystonia (DRD).
The diagnosis of GTPCH1-deficient DRD is established in a proband by identification of a heterozygous pathogenic variant in GCH1 by molecular genetic testing.
| GCH1 | GTP cyclohydrolase 1 | hgnc:4193 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in |
The diagnosis of GTPCH1-deficient DRD is established in a proband by identification of a heterozygous pathogenic variant in GCH1 by molecular genetic testing.

nodes.description: Gait ataxia is reported as frequent by Orphanet, but GeneReviews indicates that cerebellar disturbance is generally absent; this may reflect dystonic gait being recorded under an ataxia label.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0002066 | Gait ataxia | Frequent (79-30%) |
Intellectual, cerebellar, sensory, and autonomic disturbances generally do not occur.

nodes.description: Hearing impairment is listed as frequent.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0000365 | Hearing impairment | Frequent (79-30%) |

evidence.reference_title: IMPDH2: a new gene associated with dominant juvenile-onset dystonia-tremor disorder
evidence.snippet: Here we report a deleterious heterozygous truncating variant in the inosine monophosphate dehydrogenase gene (IMPDH2) by whole-exome sequencing, co-segregating with a dominantly inherited dystonia-tremor disease in a large Finnish family.
| IMPDH2 | inosine monophosphate dehydrogenase 2 | hgnc:6053 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) (loss of function) in |

nodes.description: Rare heterozygous truncating variants in IMPDH2 reduce IMP dehydrogenase function in patient cells, linking impaired guanine nucleotide metabolism to dopamine biosynthesis and autosomal dominant dystonia-tremor phenotypes.

evidence.reference_title: IMPDH2: a new gene associated with dominant juvenile-onset dystonia-tremor disorder
evidence.snippet: We show that the defect results in degradation of the gene product, causing IMPDH2 deficiency in patient cells.

evidence.reference_title: IMPDH2: a new gene associated with dominant juvenile-onset dystonia-tremor disorder
evidence.snippet: Here we report a deleterious heterozygous truncating variant in the inosine monophosphate dehydrogenase gene (IMPDH2) by whole-exome sequencing, co-segregating with a dominantly inherited dystonia-tremor disease in a large Finnish family.
| IMPDH2 | inosine monophosphate dehydrogenase 2 | hgnc:6053 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) (loss of function) in |

nodes.description: Limb dystonia is a frequent manifestation and often presents as childhood gait disturbance caused by foot dystonia.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0002451 | Limb dystonia | Frequent (79-30%) |

This disorder typically presents with gait disturbance caused by foot dystonia, later development of parkinsonism, and diurnal fluctuation of symptoms (aggravation of symptoms toward the evening and alleviation of symptoms in the morning after sleep).

evidence.reference_title: Early-onset autosomal dominant GTP-cyclohydrolase I deficiency: Diagnostic delay and residual motor signs.
evidence.snippet: gait disturbance was reported in 92.7% of patients, diurnal fluctuation of symptoms in 91.9%, and RMS in 39%.

nodes.description: Low-dose levodopa, typically with a decarboxylase inhibitor, bypasses the upstream dopamine-synthesis bottleneck and produces rapid, sustained motor improvement. Discontinuation of levodopa treatment should be avoided.

evidence.snippet: Motor benefit occurs immediately or within a few days of starting levodopa; full benefit occurs within several days to a few months. Maximum benefit (complete or near-complete responsiveness of symptoms) is generally achieved by <300-400 mg/day of levodopa/DCI.
Agents/circumstances to avoid: Discontinuation of levodopa treatment.

A rare neurometabolic disorder characterized by childhood-onset dystonia that shows a dramatic and sustained response to low doses of levodopa (L-dopa) and that may be associated with parkinsonism at an older age.

nodes.description: Lower limb hyperreflexia is frequent.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0002395 | Lower limb hyperreflexia | Frequent (79-30%) |

nodes.description: Loss-of-function variants in NR4A2 can produce early-adult dystonia parkinsonism with dopaminergic denervation on dopamine-transporter imaging, extending the autosomal dominant dopa-responsive dystonia spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Loss-of-Function Mutations in NR4A2 Cause Dopa-Responsive Dystonia Parkinsonism.
evidence.snippet: The 2 patients reported here both had a history of mild intellectual disability in childhood and subsequently developed dystonia parkinsonism in early adulthood.

evidence.reference_title: Loss-of-Function Mutations in NR4A2 Cause Dopa-Responsive Dystonia Parkinsonism.
evidence.snippet: Brain magnetic resonance imaging was normal, and DATscan suggested bilateral dopaminergic denervation.
| NR4A2 | nuclear receptor subfamily 4 group A member 2 | hgnc:7981 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) (loss of function) in |

evidence.reference_title: Loss-of-Function Mutations in NR4A2 Cause Dopa-Responsive Dystonia Parkinsonism.
evidence.snippet: Two frameshift mutations in NR4A2 were identified: a de novo insertion (NM_006186.3; c.326dupA) in the first case and another small insertion (NM_006186.3; c.881dupA) in the second.
| NR4A2 | nuclear receptor subfamily 4 group A member 2 | hgnc:7981 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) (loss of function) in |

nodes.description: Parkinsonism may develop later, especially in older patients.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0001300 | Parkinsonism | Frequent (79-30%) |

This disorder typically presents with gait disturbance caused by foot dystonia, later development of parkinsonism, and diurnal fluctuation of symptoms (aggravation of symptoms toward the evening and alleviation of symptoms in the morning after sleep).

nodes.description: Pes cavus is a frequent foot phenotype.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0001761 | Pes cavus | Frequent (79-30%) |

nodes.description: Postural tremor can occur as an initial or associated motor sign.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0002174 | Postural tremor | Frequent (79-30%) |
Occasionally, initial symptoms are arm dystonia, postural tremor of the hand, or slowness of movements.

nodes.description: Rigidity is a frequent extrapyramidal motor feature.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0002063 | Rigidity | Frequent (79-30%) |

nodes.description: Sleep abnormality is listed as frequent.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0002360 | Sleep abnormality | Frequent (79-30%) |

nodes.description: Impaired tetrahydrobiopterin-dependent catecholamine synthesis reduces dopamine production in nigrostriatal circuits, producing dystonia, parkinsonism, rigidity, and tremor that improve when levodopa supplies a downstream dopamine precursor.

evidence.reference_title: Dopa-responsive dystonia in Chinese patients: Including a novel heterozygous mutation in the GCH1 gene with an intermediate phenotype and one case of prenatal diagnosis.
evidence.snippet: DRD is caused by the mutations in the genes encoding the enzymes involved in the dopamine and tetrahydrobiopterin (BH4) biosynthesis, including the GTP cyclohydrolase 1 (GCH1) gene and the tyrosine hydroxylase (TH) gene.

GTP cyclohydrolase 1-deficient dopa-responsive dystonia (GTPCH1-deficient DRD) is characterized by childhood-onset dystonia and a dramatic and sustained response to low doses of oral administration of levodopa.

nodes.description: Talipes equinovarus is a frequent foot posture abnormality.
